mechanism_narrative: An increase in RSAFS reflects accelerating retail sales and consumer spending, which raises corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55 percent of months with month-over-month RSAFS growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5 percent signals restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns do not occur in more than 50 percent of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5 percent over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL signal accelerating nominal and real interest rate pressure that compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55 percent of months where both series increase simultaneously over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, accelerating growth in PAYEMS signals a durable rebound in employment income that restores household demand and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE elevated above 6% marks post-recession periods
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns fail to occur in more than 55 percent of months with rising PAYEMS when UNRATE is above 6 percent over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO indicate a contracting industrial sector and falling corporate output, which reduces earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55 percent of two-consecutive-decline episodes over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in RSAFS and HOUST demonstrate broad-based demand strength across consumption and housing, which lowers recession probability and lifts S&P 500 prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55 percent of months where both RSAFS and HOUST increase together over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, slowing quarter-over-quarter growth in GDPC1 signals peak aggregate demand and impending margin compression, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns do not accompany decelerating GDPC1 in more than 50 percent of late-cycle quarters over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3 percentage point or greater rise in UNRATE over a rolling 3-month window signals rapid labor market deterioration that erodes consumer spending and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55 percent of triggered 3-month windows over any 3-year rolling period.
